title: Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu

seo.title: How to Make Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu — Easy Korean Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu. This easy Korean recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu
easy Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu recipe
authentic Korean Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu

Korean Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu Recipe
h2Headings: What is Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu?

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Korean Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu — a beloved Korean classic

description: Dubu Jorim is a beloved Korean side dish featuring pan-fried tofu simmered in a savory, spicy, and slightly sweet braising sauce. This comforting vegetarian recipe offers a delightful balance of flavors and a wonderfully tender texture. It is a simple yet incredibly satisfying dish perfect for any meal alongside steamed rice.

instructions.title: Prepare and Fry the Tofu
instructions.text: Slice the pressed tofu block into 1/2-inch thick rectangles. Pat them very dry with paper towels to ensure a good sear. Heat cooking oil in a large non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Carefully place the tofu slices in a single layer and pan-fry for 3-4 minutes per side, until golden brown and slightly crispy. Remove and set aside.

instructions.title: Make the Braising Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, gochugaru, minced garlic, 2 tablespoons of minced green onion, sesame oil, sugar, and water or vegetable broth. Mix well until the sugar is dissolved and all ingredients are thoroughly combined.

instructions.title: Arrange Tofu in Pan
instructions.text: Clean the pan if necessary, or use a clean shallow pan. Arrange the pan-fried tofu slices in a single layer, ensuring they are not overlapping too much for even cooking.

instructions.title: Pour Sauce and Simmer
instructions.text: Pour the prepared braising sauce evenly over the tofu slices. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer over medium heat. Make sure all tofu pieces are coated.

instructions.title: Braise to Perfection
instructions.text: Reduce heat to low, cover the pan, and let it braise for 15-20 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly and the tofu has absorbed the rich flavors. Gently flip the tofu slices halfway through the braising process to ensure even seasoning.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Uncover the pan and let it simmer for another 2-3 minutes if the sauce is too thin, allowing it to reduce further. Garnish generously with fresh sliced green onions and toasted sesame seeds just before serving. Serve hot with freshly steamed rice as a delicious side dish or main course.

tips: Ensure your tofu is well-pressed and patted dry before frying; this prevents splattering and allows for a crispier exterior, which helps it absorb the sauce better during braising.

Adjust the spice level of the gochugaru to your preference. For a milder dish, reduce the amount, or for extra heat, add more. You can also add a pinch of black pepper for an additional layer of depth.

Dubu Jorim tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld and deepen. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. It is delicious served warm or at room temperature.
article: Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu is a beloved Korean Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
